prompt: What does this sprint burndown tell you?
caption: Dashed = ideal; solid = actual remaining work.

derived.ar: [ir[0], max(0,ir[1]+sgn*eoff*1/9), max(0,ir[2]+sgn*eoff*2/9), max(0,ir[3]+sgn*eoff*3/9), max(0,ir[4]+sgn*eoff*4/9), max(0,ir[5]+sgn*eoff*5/9), max(0,ir[6]+sgn*eoff*6/9), max(0,ir[7]+sgn*eoff*7/9), max(0,ir[8]+sgn*eoff*8/9), max(0,ir[9]+sgn*eoff)]

choices.why: 'The actual line is below ideal — less work remaining than planned.'
'The actual line is above ideal — more work remaining than planned.'
'The actual line tracks the ideal line closely.'

explanation: {{ status=='behind' ? 'The actual line sits above the ideal — work is burning down slower than planned, so the sprint is behind.' : (status=='ahead' ? 'The actual line sits below the ideal — work is burning down faster than planned, so the sprint is ahead.' : 'The actual line follows the ideal closely — the sprint is on track.') }}

prompt: At the status date PV = {{pv}}, EV = {{ev}}, AC = {{ac}}. What is the project status?
caption: PV planned · EV earned · AC actual cost.
explanation: SV = EV − PV = {{ev-pv}} ({{behind ? 'behind' : 'ahead of'}} schedule); CV = EV − AC = {{ev-ac}} ({{over ? 'over' : 'under'}} budget).

prompt: What does this run chart indicate?
caption: Solid line = mean.

derived.side: [(runLen>0 && 0>=runStart && 0<runEnd) ? above : ((runLen>0 && (0==runStart-1 || 0==runEnd)) ? (1-above) : 0), (runLen>0 && 1>=runStart && 1<runEnd) ? above : ((runLen>0 && (1==runStart-1 || 1==runEnd)) ? (1-above) : 1), (runLen>0 && 2>=runStart && 2<runEnd) ? above : ((runLen>0 && (2==runStart-1 || 2==runEnd)) ? (1-above) : 0), (runLen>0 && 3>=runStart && 3<runEnd) ? above : ((runLen>0 && (3==runStart-1 || 3==runEnd)) ? (1-above) : 1), (runLen>0 && 4>=runStart && 4<runEnd) ? above : ((runLen>0 && (4==runStart-1 || 4==runEnd)) ? (1-above) : 0), (runLen>0 && 5>=runStart && 5<runEnd) ? above : ((runLen>0 && (5==runStart-1 || 5==runEnd)) ? (1-above) : 1), (runLen>0 && 6>=runStart && 6<runEnd) ? above : ((runLen>0 && (6==runStart-1 || 6==runEnd)) ? (1-above) : 0), (runLen>0 && 7>=runStart && 7<runEnd) ? above : ((runLen>0 && (7==runStart-1 || 7==runEnd)) ? (1-above) : 1), (runLen>0 && 8>=runStart && 8<runEnd) ? above : ((runLen>0 && (8==runStart-1 || 8==runEnd)) ? (1-above) : 0), (runLen>0 && 9>=runStart && 9<runEnd) ? above : ((runLen>0 && (9==runStart-1 || 9==runEnd)) ? (1-above) : 1), (runLen>0 && 10>=runStart && 10<runEnd) ? above : ((runLen>0 && (10==runStart-1 || 10==runEnd)) ? (1-above) : 0)]

choices.why: hasSignal ? (runLen + ' consecutive points sit on the same side of the mean — the rule of seven.') : ''

!hasSignal ? (kind=='near' ? ('Only ' + runLen + ' points sit on one side — a run needs seven (the rule of seven), so this is still random.') : 'The points cross the mean regularly — no run or trend.') : ''
'A run chart has no control limits to breach.'

explanation: {{ hasSignal ? 'Seven or more consecutive points on one side of the mean is a non-random signal (the rule of seven), even with no limits breached.' : (kind=='near' ? ('A streak of ' + runLen + ' points on one side looks like a run, but the rule of seven needs at least seven — so this is still ordinary random variation.') : 'The points alternate across the mean with no run or trend — ordinary random variation.') }}
